name: Harissa Oil
directions: Using a pestle and mortar, grind the dried ingredients together to form a coarse mixture.
Add the garlic and stir in the olive oil.
Pour the mixture into a jar, allowing the spices to settle.
Keeps in the fridge for 1 week.

name: Pumpkin Orange Chili
directions: Combine all ingredients, except beans, in a large saucepan. Bring to a boil, then lower heat and let simmer for about 30 minute (stirring occasionally).
Taste, add more spices if you like.
Add beans. Simmer for 1-2 hours.
Serve and enjoy!

similar.name: Pasta Primavera With Kale, Zucchini, and Carrots

name: Spicy Vegan Chili
directions: Saute onion, garlic and peppers in olive oil for about 4 minutes until onions are translucent.  Underestimate the number of chilies you add for heat.  You can add more later if it's not hot enough for you.

Add chili powder, chipotle and sun dried tomatoes if using, dried lemon, cumin and tofu or meat subustitue and heat in oil (add additional if tofu or soy meat sticks to pan).  Cook until tofu is slightly browned, about 5 minutes.
Add remaining seasonings, Vegemite, vegetable bouillon, salt and pepper to taste and a little vegetable stock to dissolve stock base.
Once seasonings are dissolved add whole can of tomatoes, including juice, pinto beans rinsed and kidney beans rinsed and 2 cups of vegetable stock.
Bring chili to a boil adding extra liquid or broth if desired and make sure there's no stickage at the bottom of the pan.  Taste and adjust seasonings to your preference.
Return to medium low heat and cook for half an hour to an hour to allow flavors to meld and chili to thicken.
Serve with diced avocado, cilantro, green onions.

I usually serve mine with rice, cast-iron pan cornbread and sour cream.  Serving number assumes it's eaten with rice.  The rice and sour cream help cut the spice for those that don't like as much heat.  Removing seeds and membranes can lessen heat if desired.  If you like heat, try an extra chili, but watch out as it's easy to go overboard.

name: Spicy Hot Cocoa Mix
directions: Put all ingredients together in a jar or container and shake to mix. Once ingredients are mixed, use a sifter to blend and break down lumps. Store in jar or container.
Add (to taste) to a hot cup of your favorite milk or milk substitute and enjoy!
This recipe is not overly sweet, so if you like a sweeter cocoa be sure to add more sugar. The cayenne gives the drink a nice little kick!

name: Susannah's Salsa Picante
directions: Dump everything in a blender and turn it on.
Serve with tortilla chips.
Store in the refrigerator (it will keep for several days; the flavors will blend and it will get slightly hotter).

similar.name: Pasta Primavera With Kale, Zucchini, and Carrots

name: Spicy Rice
directions: Heat the oil in a large pan over medium heat. Add the scallions and garlic and cook for 5 minutes or until softened. Add the poblanos and cook for another 5 minutes.
Add the rice and cumin and cook, stirring to coat the grains in the oil, for 2 minutes.
Stir in the stock and half the chopped cilantro and bring to a boil. Reduce the heat, cover and let simmer for 15 minutes or until nearly all the liquid has been absorbed and the rice is just tender.
Remove from heat and fluff up with a fork. Stir in the remaining cilantro and season to taste with salt and pepper. Let stand, covered for 5 minutes before serving.

similar.name: Pasta Primavera With Kale, Zucchini, and Carrots

name: Pasta Primavera With Kale, Zucchini, and Carrots
directions: Start by cooking pasta per package directions and set aside to drain and cool. Add EVOO to pasta if you like to keep it from getting sticky.
In a large 3-4 qt pan, heat EVOO to medium heat and sauté onions, red pepper and garlic together until soft.
Add crushed tomatoes, then carrots and kale.
Stir and let it come to a slow boil, then turn the heat down to medium low for about 30 minutes.
Start on the zucchini. Preheat oven to 350 degrees.
Coat each side of the zucchini with breadcrumb mixture and place on a oven proof dish. Use an oil spray on dish to keep it from sticking. Bake for 10-15 minutes.
Add fresh herbs to red sauce, and let it cook for another 15 minutes.
Finish primavera sauce with cream and add salt to taste.
Toss pasta with red sauce to coat. Top each serving with 2 pieces of baked zucchini and grated parmesean cheese and enjoy!
